title: {name} beim Zahnarzt
moral: Bravery and self-care make us stronger

beats.beat: Title page — {name} smiling wide, showing teeth, toothbrush in hand
Morning at home — {name} notices a wobbly tooth while eating breakfast with {parent}
{parent} explains today is dentist day — {name} feels a little nervous
Driving through {city} to the dentist — {name} holds {companion} tightly in the car
Arriving at the dental practice — colorful building with a big tooth sign
Waiting room — fish tank, children's books, a toy corner; {name} explores
The friendly dentist greets {name} — white coat, warm smile, introduces the chair
Sitting in the big chair — it goes up and down like a spaceship; {name} giggles
The dentist shows the tools — mirror, explorer, air puffer; {name} is curious
{name} feels a little scared — squeezes {companion} for courage
The dentist counts {name}'s teeth — {name} opens wide and counts along
The little mirror goes inside — {name} sees teeth on a screen, amazed
Air puffer tickles — {name} laughs and the dentist laughs too
Time for polishing — the electric brush buzzes and {name} is brave
Choosing the tooth polish flavor — strawberry, mint, or bubblegum
Rinsing and spitting — {name} practices with the little water fountain
The dentist says teeth are healthy — gives a big thumbs up
{name} gets a bravery sticker and a new toothbrush as reward
Saying goodbye to the dentist — {name} waves proudly
Walking out with {parent} — {name} shows the sticker, beaming with pride
Telling {parent} all about the chair, the mirror, and the buzzy brush
At home — {name} shows the sticker to the whole family
Bedtime routine — {name} brushes teeth extra carefully with the new toothbrush
{name} in bed, telling {companion} about being brave at the dentist, drifting off to sleep

vocabulary_constraints.toddler: max 8 words per sentence, 3-4 sentences per page
vocabulary_constraints.preschool: max 12 words per sentence, 4-5 sentences per page
vocabulary_constraints.early-reader: max 15 words per sentence, 5-7 sentences per page
